docs.title: Beamable Web SDK
docs.text:
The Beamable SDK provides functionality to integrate your game with the Beamable features. These range from Content (Remote Config) to Events to Inventory to Microservices (Beamable's unique approach to a Cloud Code Solution).

  • Getting Started

    Install Web SDK and get up and running in minutes

    Getting started

  • Web SDK

    Check our User Reference of the Beamable Web SDK

    Web SDK

docs.title: Beam Web SDK
docs.text: Beam Web SDK is a library built with TypeScript that can be used in both Node.js and browser environments. It is distributed in multiple module formats (ESM, CommonJS, IIFE) and provides full TypeScript declarations.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install the SDK into your project via npm (or pnpm):

# npm
npm install beamable-sdk
# pnpm
pnpm add beamable-sdk

docs.title: Using the SDK
docs.text: You can use the Beam SDK across different JavaScript environments:

docs.title: CommonJS (Node.js)
docs.text:
const { Beam } = require('beamable-sdk');
const beam = await Beam.init({
  cid: 'YOUR_CUSTOMER_ID',
  pid: 'YOUR_PROJECT_ID',
});
console.log(beam.player.id);

docs.title: ES Modules (Node.js)
docs.text:
import { Beam } from 'beamable-sdk';
const beam = await Beam.init({
  cid: 'YOUR_CUSTOMER_ID',
  pid: 'YOUR_PROJECT_ID',
});
console.log(beam.player.id);

docs.title: Browser (IIFE)
docs.text:
<script src="https://unpkg.com/beamable-sdk"></script>
<script>
  // global variable exposed as Beamable
  const { Beam } = Beamable;
  const beam = await Beam.init({
    cid: 'YOUR_CUSTOMER_ID',
    pid: 'YOUR_PROJECT_ID',
  });
  console.log(beam.player.id);
</script>

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
Find detailed API references, usage examples, and integration guides for the Beam Web SDK:

Beam Web SDK Documentation

docs.title: Token storage and automatic refresh
docs.text: The SDK stores authentication tokens using a platform-appropriate strategy and automatically handles access token renewal when needed.

docs.title: Automatic refresh
docs.text: All HTTP requests that receive a 401 response will trigger an automatic access token refresh using the stored refresh token. On successful refresh, the original request is retried with the new access token. If the refresh fails, stored tokens are cleared and the error is propagated.

docs.title: Refresh failures
docs.text:
If the SDK fails to refresh the access token (for example, the refresh token has expired), it throws a RefreshAccessTokenError and stored tokens are cleared. Consumers should catch this error to start a new login flow:

import { RefreshAccessTokenError } from 'beamable-sdk';

try {
  await beam.someService.someMethod();
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof RefreshAccessTokenError) {
    // Refresh failed: redirect user to login
    redirectToLoginPage();
  }
}

docs.title: Custom token storage
docs.text:
If you need a different storage mechanism, implement the TokenStorage interface and pass it in the configuration:

import { TokenStorage } from 'beamable-sdk';

class MyTokenStorage implements TokenStorage {
  /* implement getTokenData, setTokenData, ... */
}

const beam = await Beam.init({
  cid: 'YOUR_CUSTOMER_ID',
  pid: 'YOUR_PROJECT_ID',
  tokenStorage: new MyTokenStorage(),
});

docs.title: License
docs.text: This project is licensed under the MIT License.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
Contributions are welcome! This project is configured with:

  • pnpm as the package manager.

  • TypeScript for compile-time type safety.

  • tsdown for bundling the code into multiple formats:

  • esm
  • cjs

  • iife (for CDN usage)

  • Vitest as the test runner.

  • ESLint/Prettier for maintaining code quality and style.

  • TypeDoc for SDK documentation.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository.

  2. Run the setup script (recommended):

cd web
npm run setup-project  # Checks node/pnpm versions and installs dependencies

If you prefer manual setup, ensure you are using node version >= 22.14.0 and pnpm version 10.8.0

cd web
npm i -g pnpm@10.8.0 # If you don't have pnpm installed
pnpm install

  1. Create a new feature branch.

  2. Make your changes and commit with clear messages.

  3. Open a pull request with a description of your changes.

  4. Before submitting a PR, make sure to format, lint, and test: 

    pnpm format   # Format code
pnpm lint     # Run linter
pnpm test     # Run test suite
pnpm dev      # Run development build

docs.title: Testing SDK Builds Locally
docs.text: To test the local SDK builds across different module formats by creating temporary files in a temp folder and importing from the dist directory. This ensures the bundle works correctly in Node.js (CommonJS, ESM) and in the browser (IIFE).

docs.title: CommonJS (Node.js)
docs.text:
Create a file temp/test-cjs.js:

const { Beam } = require('./dist/index.js');
const beam = await Beam.init({
  cid: 'YOUR_CUSTOMER_ID',
  pid: 'YOUR_PROJECT_ID',
});
console.log(beam.player.id);

Run:

node test-cjs.js

docs.title: ES Modules (Node.js)
docs.text:
Create a file temp/test-esm.mjs:

import { Beam } from './dist/index.mjs';
const beam = await Beam.init({
  cid: 'YOUR_CUSTOMER_ID',
  pid: 'YOUR_PROJECT_ID',
});
console.log(beam.player.id);

Run:

node test-esm.mjs

docs.title: Browser (IIFE)
docs.text:
Create an HTML file temp/test-iife.html:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <title>Beam SDK Test</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <!-- Load the IIFE bundle; exposes a global `Beam` symbol -->
    <script src="../dist/index.global.js"></script>

    <script>
      // Instantiate the SDK via the `Beam` constructor
      const { Beam, BeamEnvironment } = Beamable;
      const beam = await Beam.init({
        cid: 'YOUR_CUSTOMER_ID',
        pid: 'YOUR_PROJECT_ID',
        // Environment must be one of: 'Dev', 'Stg', or 'Prod'
        environment: 'Prod',
      });

      // List all built-in environments:
      console.log(BeamEnvironment.list());

      // Display current player id:
      console.log(beam.player.id);
    </script>
  </body>
</html>

Open the file in your browser and watch the console for output.

docs.title: Releasing & Versioning
docs.text:
Please ensure that all notable changes are clearly documented in the CHANGELOG.md file before releasing.

When we are ready to release, update the version and build:

pnpm release # This updates the version and build the project
pnpm publish --access public

docs.title: Interface: BeamConfig
docs.text:
Defined in: src/configs/BeamConfig.ts:5

Configuration options for initializing the Beam Client SDK.

docs.title: contentNamespaces?
docs.text:
optional contentNamespaces: string[]

Defined in: src/configs/BeamBaseConfig.ts:33

List of content namespaces to load. By default, only 'global' is loaded.

docs.title: environment?
docs.text:
optional environment: BeamEnvironmentName

Defined in: src/configs/BeamBaseConfig.ts:18

The Beamable environment to connect to. Can be one of 'prod', 'stg', 'dev', or a custom environment name.

docs.title: gameEngine?
docs.text:
optional gameEngine: string

Defined in: src/configs/BeamConfig.ts:7

Name of the game engine (e.g., "Three.js", "Phaser", "Babylon", "PlayCanvas").

docs.title: gameEngineVersion?
docs.text:
optional gameEngineVersion: string

Defined in: src/configs/BeamConfig.ts:10

Version of the game engine.

docs.title: gameVersion?
docs.text:
optional gameVersion: string

Defined in: src/configs/BeamBaseConfig.ts:30

Published version of the game.

docs.title: instanceTag?

docs.title: stackTraceLimit
docs.text:
static stackTraceLimit: number

Defined in: node_modules/.pnpm/@types+node@22.19.1/node_modules/@types/node/globals.d.ts:68

The Error.stackTraceLimit property specifies the number of stack frames collected by a stack trace (whether generated by new Error().stack or Error.captureStackTrace(obj)).

The default value is 10 but may be set to any valid JavaScript number. Changes will affect any stack trace captured after the value has been changed.

If set to a non-number value, or set to a negative number, stack traces will not capture any frames.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text:
Record<string, unknown>

A plain object suitable for logging or serializing.

docs.title: captureStackTrace()
docs.text:
static captureStackTrace(targetObject, constructorOpt?): void

Defined in: node_modules/.pnpm/@types+node@22.19.1/node_modules/@types/node/globals.d.ts:52

Creates a .stack property on targetObject, which when accessed returns a string representing the location in the code at which Error.captureStackTrace() was called.

const myObject = {};
Error.captureStackTrace(myObject);
myObject.stack;  // Similar to `new Error().stack`

The first line of the trace will be prefixed with ${myObject.name}: ${myObject.message}.

The optional constructorOpt argument accepts a function. If given, all frames above constructorOpt, including constructorOpt, will be omitted from the generated stack trace.

The constructorOpt argument is useful for hiding implementation details of error generation from the user. For instance:

function a() {
  b();
}

function b() {
  c();
}

function c() {
  // Create an error without stack trace to avoid calculating the stack trace twice.
  const { stackTraceLimit } = Error;
  Error.stackTraceLimit = 0;
  const error = new Error();
  Error.stackTraceLimit = stackTraceLimit;

  // Capture the stack trace above function b
  Error.captureStackTrace(error, b); // Neither function c, nor b is included in the stack trace
  throw error;
}

a();

docs.title: Interface: ContentBase<T>
docs.text:
Defined in: src/contents/types/ContentBase.ts:5

Base interface for content types. This interface defines the common structure for all content types in the system.

docs.title: Interface: ContentTypeMap
docs.text:
Defined in: src/contents/types/ContentTypeMap.ts:18

Define a mapping of content prefixes to their corresponding types

docs.title: Type Alias: ContentTypeFromId<Id>
docs.text:
ContentTypeFromId<Id> = Id extends keyof ContentTypeMap ? ContentTypeMap[Id] : ExtractPrefixes<Id> extends infer Prefixes ? Prefixes extends keyof ContentTypeMap ? ContentTypeMap[Prefixes] : ContentBase : ContentBase

Defined in: src/contents/types/ContentTypeFromId.ts:40

Derives the most specific TypeScript type for a given content ID.

This utility searches for a type by first attempting an exact match on the ID. If no direct match is found, it falls back to checking the parent ID (the string with its last segment removed). If no match is found, it defaults to the base ContentBase type.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// Assuming ContentTypeMap contains:
// "items": ItemContent
// "items.shield": ShieldContent
// "items.shield.wooden": WoodenShieldContent

type T1 = ContentTypeFromId<"items.shield.wooden.rare">; // -> WoodenShieldContent
type T2 = ContentTypeFromId<"items.shield.metal">; // -> ShieldContent
type T3 = ContentTypeFromId<"items.weapon">; // -> ItemContent
type T4 = ContentTypeFromId<"unknown.type">; // -> ContentBase

Defined in: src/core/Beam.ts:269

Unsubscribes from a specific context or removes all subscriptions if no handler is provided.

The callback to remove. If not provided, all handlers for the context are removed.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
beam.off('inventory.refresh', handler);
// or to remove all handlers for the context
beam.off('inventory.refresh');

docs.title: on()

The callback to process the data when a message is received.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
const handler = (data) => {
  console.log('New inventory data:', data);
}
beam.use(InventoryService);
beam.on('inventory.refresh', handler);

docs.title: refresh()
docs.text:
refresh(tokenResponse?): Promise<void>

Defined in: src/core/Beam.ts:193

Refreshes the current Beam SDK instance with a new token response. This method re-initializes the SDK with the provided token, updates the internal state, and re-establishes necessary connections.

docs.title: tokenResponse?
docs.text:
TokenResponse

The new token response to use for refreshing the SDK.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
const newToken = await beam.auth.loginWithEmail({ email, password });
await beam.refresh(newToken);

Defined in: src/core/BeamServer.ts:168

Unsubscribes from a specific server-event or removes all subscriptions if no handler is provided.

docs.title: handler?
docs.text:
(data) => void

The callback to remove. If not provided, all handlers for the server-event are removed.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
beamServer.off('content.manifest', handler);
// or to remove all handlers for the server-event
beamServer.off('content.manifest');

docs.title: on()
docs.text:
on<K>(eventType, handler): void

Defined in: src/core/BeamServer.ts:137

Subscribes to a server-event and listens for messages.

docs.title: handler
docs.text:
(data) => void

The callback to process the data when a message is received.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
const handler = (data) => {
  console.log('Content manifest received:', data);
};
beamServer.on('content.manifest', handler);

docs.title: Call Signature
docs.text:
use<T>(ctors): this

Defined in: src/core/BeamServer.ts:65

Dynamically adds multiple api services or microservice clients to the Beam SDK.

docs.title: ctors
docs.text:
readonly T[]

An array of constructors for the api service or microservice client.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
const beam = await Beam.init({ ... });
beam.use([LeadboardService, StatsService]);
or 
const beam = await Beam.init({ ... });
beam.use([MyMicroserviceClient, MyOtherMicroserviceClient]);

docs.title: Call Signature
docs.text:
use<T>(ctor): this

Defined in: src/core/BeamServer.ts:68

Dynamically adds a single api service or microservice client to the Beam SDK.

docs.title: ctor
docs.text:
T

The constructor for the api service or microservice client.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
const beam = await Beam.init({ ... });
beam.use(StatsService);
or 
const beam = await Beam.init({ ... });
beam.use(MyMicroserviceClient);

docs.title: addCredentials()
docs.text:
addCredentials(params): Promise<AccountPlayerView>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:100

Registers a new account with the given email and password, and associates it with the current player.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const account = beam.account;
// server-side:
const account = beamServer.account(playerId);
// register a new account
const playerAccount = await account.addCredentials({
  email: 'player@example.com',
  password: 'password123',
});

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the registration fails (e.g., email already exists, invalid password).

docs.title: addExternalIdentity()
docs.text:
addExternalIdentity(params): Promise<AccountPlayerView>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:175

Links an external identity to the current player's Beamable account.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const account = beam.account;
// server-side:
const account = beamServer.account(playerId);
// link an external identity
const playerAccount = await account.addExternalIdentity({
  externalToken: 'acme-auth-token',
  providerService: acmeServiceClient.serviceName,
  providerNamespace: acmeServiceClient.federationIds.acme,
  // optional
  challengeHandler: (challenge) => {
    // Handle the challenge, e.g., by displaying a CAPTCHA or OTP to the user
    return prompt(challenge);
  },
});

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the linking fails (e.g., invalid token, account already linked).

docs.title: addThirdParty()
docs.text:
addThirdParty(params): Promise<AccountPlayerView>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:134

Associates a third-party account with the current player's Beamable account.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const account = beam.account;
// server-side:
const account = beamServer.account(playerId);
// add third-party credentials
const playerAccount = await account.addThirdParty({
  provider: ThirdPartyAuthProvider.Google,
  token: 'google-auth-token',
});

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the association fails (e.g., invalid token, account already linked).

docs.title: confirmEmailUpdate()
docs.text:
confirmEmailUpdate(params): Promise<void>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:445

Confirms an email update process for the current player's account using a provided confirmation code and password.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const account = beam.account;
// server-side:
const account = beamServer.account(playerId);
// confirm email update
await account.confirmEmailUpdate({
  code: 'confirmation-code',
  password: 'current-password',
});

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the confirmation fails (e.g., invalid code, incorrect password).

docs.title: confirmPasswordUpdate()
docs.text:
confirmPasswordUpdate(params): Promise<void>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:495

Confirms a password update process for the current player's account using a provided confirmation code and new password.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const account = beam.account;
// server-side:
const account = beamServer.account(playerId);
// confirm password update
await account.confirmPasswordUpdate({
  code: 'confirmation-code',
  newPassword: 'new-password',
});

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the confirmation fails (e.g., invalid code, invalid new password).

docs.title: current()
docs.text:
current(): Promise<AccountPlayerView>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:75

Fetches the current player's account information.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const playerAccount = await beam.account.current();
// server-side:
const playerAccount = await beamServer.account(playerId).current();

docs.title: getEmailCredentialStatus()
docs.text:
getEmailCredentialStatus(params): Promise<CredentialStatus>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:315

Checks the status of an email credential.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const account = beam.account;
// server-side:
const account = beamServer.account(playerId);
// check the status of an email credential
const status = await account.getEmailCredentialStatus({
  email: 'player@example.com',
});
if (status === CredentialStatus.NotAssigned) {
  console.log('Email credential not assigned.');
}

docs.title: getExternalIdentityStatus()
docs.text:
getExternalIdentityStatus(params): Promise<CredentialStatus>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:387

Checks the status of an external identity credential.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const account = beam.account;
// server-side:
const account = beamServer.account(playerId);
// check the status of an external identity credential
const status = await account.getExternalIdentityStatus({
  providerService: acmeServiceClient.serviceName,
  providerNamespace: acmeServiceClient.federationIds.acme,
  externalUserId: 'acme-user-id',
});
if (status === CredentialStatus.NotAssigned) {
  console.log('External identity credential not assigned.');
}

docs.title: getThirdPartyStatus()
docs.text:
getThirdPartyStatus(params): Promise<CredentialStatus>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:350

Checks the status of a third-party credential.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const account = beam.account;
// server-side:
const account = beamServer.account(playerId);
// check the status of a third-party credential
const status = await account.getThirdPartyStatus({
  provider: ThirdPartyAuthProvider.Google,
  token: 'google-auth-token',
});
if (status === CredentialStatus.NotAssigned) {
  console.log('Google credential not assigned.');
}

docs.title: initiateEmailUpdate()
docs.text:
initiateEmailUpdate(params): Promise<void>

Defined in: src/services/AccountService.ts:421

Defined in: src/services/AnnouncementsService.ts:85

// server-side:

// claim multiple announcements
await announcements.claim({ id: ["id-1", "id-2"] });

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the announcement ID is invalid or the operation fails.

Defined in: src/services/ContentService.ts:162

Retrieves content by its ID.

docs.title: Remarks
docs.text: This method first checks the in-memory cache, then persistent storage, and finally fetches from the API if not found or if the content is outdated.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
const item = await beam.content.getById({
  id: 'items.my_item',
  manifestId: 'global', // Optional, defaults to 'global'
});
console.log(item.properties);

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the content is not found or cannot be retrieved.

docs.title: get()
docs.text:
get(params): Promise<LeaderBoardView>

Defined in: src/services/LeaderboardsService.ts:103

Fetches a leaderboard view for the current player.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side:
const leaderboards = beam.leaderboards;
// server-side:
const leaderboards = beamServer.leaderboards(playerId);
// fetch a leaderboard view
const leaderboardView = await leaderboards.get({
  id: 'leaderboard-id',
  from: 1, // optional, to start from a specific rank
  max: 10, // optional, to limit the number of ranks returned
  focus: 'playerGamertagOrId', // optional, to center the view on a specific player
  outlier: 'anotherPlayerGamertagOrId', // optional, to include another player as an outlier
  includeFriends: true, // optional, to include friends in the view
  includeGuilds: true, // optional, to include group members in the view
});

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the request fails or the leaderboard does not exist.

docs.title: get()
docs.text:
get(params): Promise<Record<string, string>>

Defined in: src/services/StatsService.ts:78

Fetches stats for the current player.

docs.title: Remarks
docs.text: Game domain stats can only be fetched by the game server.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// client-side: fetch private or public stats for a client domain
const stats = await beam.stats.get({
  accessType: 'private', // or 'public'
  stats: ['CURRENT_LEVEL', 'SCORE'], // optional, fetches all stats if not provided
});
// server-side: fetch private or public stats for a game domain
const gameStats = await beamServer.stats(playerId).get({
  domainType: 'game',
  accessType: 'private', // or 'public'
  stats: ['CURRENT_LEVEL', 'SCORE'], // optional, fetches all stats if not provided
});

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the request fails or the stats do not exist.

docs.title: set()
docs.text:
set(params): Promise<void>

Defined in: src/services/StatsService.ts:134

Sets a stats for the current player.

docs.title: Remarks
docs.text: Game domain stats can only be set by the game server.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
const stats = {
  CURRENT_LEVEL: '10',
  SCORE: '1000',
};
// client-side: set stats for a client domain
await beam.stats.set({
  accessType: 'private', // or 'public'
  stats,
});
// server-side: set stats for a game domain
await beamServer.stats(playerId).set({
  domainType: 'game',
  accessType: 'private', // or 'public'
  stats,
});

docs.title: Throws
docs.text: If the request fails or the stats cannot be set.

docs.title: Version 1.0
docs.text: This is the release notes for the Web version 1.0

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Enable Beam SDK configs to register all client/server services during initialization.
  • Beam.use and BeamServer.use now accept arrays of services or microservice clients for batch registration.

docs.title: Fixes
docs.text: • Skip refresh token attempts when endpoints return known BeamRequester errors (InvalidCredentialsError, InvalidRefreshTokenError, TokenValidationError).
